title: valantia hispida var. hispida
intro: Valantia hispida var. hispida, also known as prickly valerian or hairy valerian, is a flowering plant species in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is characterized by its hairy stems and yellowish-green flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties. The health benefits of Valantia hispida var. hispida include reducing anxiety and insomnia, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antispasmodic, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal effects.
